terms.definition: Antihypertensive class blocking angiotensin-converting enzyme; lowers BP and protects renal function in diabetics.
terms.exampleSentence: Counsel the patient that ramipril, an ACE inhibitor, may cause a persistent dry cough in the first weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Check U&Es within 1-2 weeks of initiation per BNF.

terms.definition: Antihypertensive class blocking AT1 receptors; alternative to ACE inhibitors when cough is intolerable.
terms.exampleSentence: We switched her from ramipril to losartan, an angiotensin II receptor blocker, after she developed a dry cough.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid combining ARB with ACE inhibitor due to hyperkalaemia risk.

terms.definition: Drug class antagonising beta-adrenergic receptors; used in hypertension, angina, arrhythmia and heart failure.
terms.exampleSentence: Bisoprolol, a cardioselective beta blocker, is indicated for stable chronic heart failure.
terms.contextNotes: Caution in asthma; avoid abrupt withdrawal.

terms.definition: Drug class blocking L-type calcium channels; used for hypertension and angina (e.g. amlodipine, diltiazem).
terms.exampleSentence: Amlodipine, a calcium channel blocker, can cause ankle swelling in some patients.
terms.contextNotes: Verapamil is contraindicated with beta blockers due to bradycardia risk.

terms.definition: Diuretic acting on the distal tubule; first-line antihypertensive in some patients (e.g. indapamide, bendroflumethiazide).
terms.exampleSentence: Indapamide, a thiazide-like diuretic, is preferred in patients over 55 without diabetes per NICE.
terms.contextNotes: Monitor sodium, potassium and urate.

terms.definition: Potent diuretic acting on the loop of Henle (e.g. furosemide); used in heart failure and pulmonary oedema.
terms.exampleSentence: She was given IV furosemide, a loop diuretic, to relieve acute pulmonary oedema.
terms.contextNotes: Causes hypokalaemia; monitor U&Es.

terms.definition: Diuretic that retains potassium (e.g. spironolactone, amiloride); often combined with thiazide or loop agents.
terms.exampleSentence: Spironolactone, a potassium-sparing diuretic, is added in resistant hypertension and chronic heart failure.
terms.contextNotes: Risk of hyperkalaemia, especially with ACE inhibitor or ARB.

terms.definition: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor that lowers LDL cholesterol; used in primary and secondary CV prevention.
terms.exampleSentence: Atorvastatin 20 mg nocte, a statin, is offered for primary prevention when QRISK3 is ≥10%.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel on muscle pain; check baseline LFTs and CK if symptomatic.

terms.definition: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug; analgesic, antipyretic, anti-inflammatory (e.g. ibuprofen, naproxen).
terms.exampleSentence: Avoid prescribing an NSAID such as ibuprofen for patients on warfarin without gastric protection.
terms.contextNotes: GI, renal and CV risks; co-prescribe a PPI in higher-risk patients.

terms.definition: Analgesic class acting on mu, kappa and delta receptors; used for moderate to severe pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Counsel that codeine, a weak opioid, may cause constipation and drowsiness.
terms.contextNotes: Many are CD Schedule 2; safe-storage advice required.

terms.definition: First-line simple analgesic and antipyretic; max 4 g daily in adults of normal weight.
terms.exampleSentence: Take one to two paracetamol 500 mg tablets up to four times a day, no more than eight in twenty-four hours.
terms.contextNotes: Reduce dose if body weight <50 kg; hepatotoxic in overdose.

terms.definition: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor; first-line antidepressant class (e.g. sertraline, citalopram).
terms.exampleSentence: Sertraline, an SSRI, is generally first-line for moderate depression in primary care.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel on initial worsening of anxiety and suicidality risk in under-25s.

terms.definition: Serotonin-noradrenaline reuptake inhibitor; antidepressant class (e.g. venlafaxine, duloxetine).
terms.exampleSentence: Venlafaxine, an SNRI, may be considered if an SSRI has not produced adequate response.
terms.contextNotes: Monitor BP at higher doses of venlafaxine.

terms.definition: Older antidepressant class (e.g. amitriptyline); also used in low dose for neuropathic pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Low-dose amitriptyline, a tricyclic antidepressant, is often prescribed for neuropathic pain at night.
terms.contextNotes: Highly toxic in overdose; anticholinergic side effects.

terms.definition: First-generation antipsychotic acting primarily on D2 receptors (e.g. haloperidol, chlorpromazine).
terms.exampleSentence: Haloperidol, a typical antipsychotic, carries a higher risk of extrapyramidal side effects than newer agents.
terms.contextNotes: Watch for tardive dyskinesia on long-term use.

terms.definition: Second-generation antipsychotic with broader receptor profile (e.g. olanzapine, risperidone, quetiapine).
terms.exampleSentence: Olanzapine, an atypical antipsychotic, is associated with weight gain and metabolic syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Annual physical health check including HbA1c and lipids.

terms.definition: Vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant; requires INR monitoring and dose titration.
terms.exampleSentence: Bring your yellow anticoagulant book to every appointment so we can check your INR on warfarin.
terms.contextNotes: Many drug and food interactions; counsel on consistent leafy-green intake.

terms.definition: Direct oral anticoagulant (e.g. apixaban, rivaroxaban); fixed-dose, no routine INR monitoring.
terms.exampleSentence: Apixaban, a DOAC, is now preferred over warfarin for most patients with non-valvular AF.
terms.contextNotes: Dose-adjust by renal function, age and weight per BNF.

terms.definition: Drug that inhibits platelet aggregation (e.g. aspirin, clopidogrel); used in CV secondary prevention.
terms.exampleSentence: Following his MI, he was discharged on dual antiplatelet therapy with aspirin and ticagrelor.
terms.contextNotes: Bleeding risk; assess for GI protection with PPI.

terms.definition: Proton pump inhibitor; suppresses gastric acid (e.g. omeprazole, lansoprazole).
terms.exampleSentence: Take omeprazole, a PPI, half an hour before food for best acid suppression.
terms.contextNotes: Long-term use linked to hypomagnesaemia and B12 deficiency.

terms.definition: Histamine H2-receptor blocker that reduces gastric acid (e.g. famotidine).
terms.exampleSentence: Famotidine, an H2 antagonist, may be used when a PPI is unsuitable for reflux symptoms.
terms.contextNotes: Ranitidine was withdrawn in 2020 due to NDMA contamination.

terms.definition: Steroid hormone class with anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects (e.g. prednisolone).
terms.exampleSentence: Take prednisolone, a corticosteroid, with breakfast and never stop suddenly after long-term use.
terms.contextNotes: Issue a steroid emergency card for long-term users.

terms.definition: Short-acting beta-2 agonist bronchodilator for acute asthma relief (e.g. salbutamol).
terms.exampleSentence: Use your salbutamol inhaler, a SABA, when you feel short of breath, up to four times a day.
terms.contextNotes: Using more than three SABA inhalers a year suggests poor control.

terms.definition: Long-acting beta-2 agonist bronchodilator (e.g. salmeterol, formoterol); used with an inhaled steroid.
terms.exampleSentence: She was started on a combined ICS-LABA inhaler for persistent asthma symptoms.
terms.contextNotes: LABA monotherapy in asthma is contraindicated.

terms.definition: Short-acting muscarinic antagonist bronchodilator (e.g. ipratropium); used in COPD and acute asthma.
terms.exampleSentence: Nebulised ipratropium, a SAMA, was added to salbutamol during the acute asthma attack.
terms.contextNotes: Stop SAMA when LAMA is started to avoid duplication.

terms.definition: Long-acting muscarinic antagonist bronchodilator (e.g. tiotropium); maintenance therapy in COPD.
terms.exampleSentence: Tiotropium, a LAMA, is taken once daily via the HandiHaler device.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel on dry mouth and urinary retention.

terms.definition: H1-receptor antagonist used in allergy and urticaria; non-sedating preferred (e.g. cetirizine, loratadine).
terms.exampleSentence: A non-sedating antihistamine such as loratadine is recommended for daytime hay-fever symptoms.
terms.contextNotes: Sedating antihistamines (e.g. chlorphenamine) impair driving.

terms.definition: Oral antidiabetic class (metformin); first-line in type 2 diabetes.
terms.exampleSentence: Metformin, a biguanide, should be taken with or just after meals to reduce GI side effects.
terms.contextNotes: Withhold during acute illness with risk of dehydration.

terms.definition: Oral antidiabetic class stimulating insulin secretion (e.g. gliclazide); risk of hypoglycaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Counsel patients on gliclazide, a sulfonylurea, about recognising and treating hypoglycaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in older frail patients due to hypo risk.

terms.definition: Alpha-1 adrenergic antagonist; used in benign prostatic hyperplasia and resistant hypertension (e.g. tamsulosin).
terms.exampleSentence: Tamsulosin, an alpha blocker, can cause postural hypotension, especially with the first dose.
terms.contextNotes: Take the first dose at bedtime to reduce syncope risk.
